source: GBIF.org occurrence counts (live snapshot). Map tiles: GBIF occurrence density.
note: GBIF holds ~3.9 billion dated, located records of where life has been observed. Every record rolls up into one of eight kingdoms; ~14.5 M are identified only to 'life' (no kingdom). Coverage is wildly uneven — birds alone are ~2.35 B (60%), a birdwatching effect, not because birds outnumber insects. Humans are recorded too (Homo sapiens), but GBIF restricts human occurrences for privacy, so despite 8 billion of us only ~24 k records exist.

species.note: Wetland wading bird expanding north as winters warm — a visible marker of range shift.
Warmth-loving bird now breeding far north of its former Mediterranean range.
One of the fastest range-expanding butterflies as the climate warms.
Pollinator whose range and phenology are shifting with temperature.
Fish stock shifting poleward with ocean warming, straining fishery treaties.
Sea-ice-dependent breeder; a climate-vulnerability icon of Antarctica.
Reef-building coral acutely sensitive to marine heatwaves and bleaching.
Cold-adapted mammal squeezed by the northward push of the red fox.
